_comment: Gauntlet safety wiring (FR-7.3, plan P2). The PreToolUse hook routes every tool call through the localhost judge via the gauntlet-judge-hook console script (installed on PATH by `uv tool install` / `pipx install`, FR-1.1; `gauntlet init` writes this file, FR-1.2). The hook is safe-by-default: it gates ONLY gauntlet-run sessions, keyed on GAUNTLET_RUN_ID (engine-injected for the run's lifetime, cleared on stop). Outside a run — i.e. any plain interactive session, even one with a GAUNTLET_JUDGE_TOKEN exported globally in ~/.zshenv — it emits no permission decision and defers to the CLI's normal permission handling, so the hook behaves as if absent: never bricked, never force-prompted, regardless of whether a judge is reachable. Under an active gauntlet run the engine sets GAUNTLET_RUN_ID/JUDGE_TOKEN/URL/MODE; unattended runs fail closed on a dead judge, interactive runs fall back to a prompt (review F-004).
